proves: the checked manifest has one declared owner for each required shared resource
output is represented only as the required primary pass color and is not a selected named MRT output
depth is represented as the primary pass depth texture rather than an MRT color output
normal, albedo, emissive, and velocity are conditional named MRT candidates rather than a required attachment set
the declared exposure, tone-map, grading, and output-conversion order has one graph owner
doesNotProve: that a WebGPURenderer or RenderPipeline was constructed, compiled, or rendered
that any conditional MRT candidate should be selected for a concrete workload
physical attachment formats, allocation, aliasing, bandwidth, memory, or timing
temporal velocity coverage, rejection, jitter, reset behavior, or history quality
exposure-meter accuracy, adaptation behavior, tone-map appearance, LUT correctness, or output pixels
browser, GPU, mobile, or low-end-device acceptance

numericProvenance.rule: Every numeric literal in this manifest is carried by a record with a Derived, Gated, Measured, or Authored tag.

tagDefinitions.Derived: follows from a formula, data layout, or verified API consequence
tagDefinitions.Gated: valid only after a named capability or correctness gate
tagDefinitions.Measured: captured on a named target and complete graph
tagDefinitions.Authored: a deliberate contract or quality starting point, not a performance result

rendererOwnerCount.basis: single-owner integration constraint; not a runtime observation

renderPipelineOwnerCount.basis: single-owner integration constraint; not a runtime observation

primaryScenePassOwnerCount.basis: single primary scene-pass owner is the baseline architecture, not a runtime observation; extra traversals need separate measured justification

normal.selectOnlyWhen: [Measured] attachment export, store, and read cost is lower than depth reconstruction or a narrow alternative on the named target and complete graph

albedo.producerContract: map diffuseColor.rgb to the named albedo output explicitly
albedo.selectOnlyWhen: [Measured] a concrete composite consumes it and its attachment cost is lower than reconstruction or a narrow alternative

emissive.selectOnlyWhen: [Measured] selective bloom or another declared consumer beats deriving its signal from primary HDR color

velocity.convention: current-minus-previous NDC with consumer-specific NDC-to-UV conversion
velocity.selectOnlyWhen: [Measured] an enabled temporal consumer justifies the attachment and rigid, instanced, skinned, and procedural deformation coverage is validated

selectionPolicy.rule: select each named MRT candidate independently for the concrete workload
selectionPolicy.costComparison: costMRT(signal) versus reconstruction or narrow-rerender cost
selectionPolicy.requiredEvidence: paired measurements on the named browser, GPU, physical pixels, and complete enabled graph
selectionPolicy.tileGpuNote: saved traversal does not imply lower off-chip bandwidth on a tile-based GPU

requires: reset or rebuild policy for discontinuities
temporalHistory.defaultOrder: resolve stable scene radiance before the exposure meter tap and bloom

exposureControl.meterSelection: fixed, stratified, exact reduction, reusable pyramid, or histogram according to the workload decision tree
exposureControl.cpuReadbackPolicy: diagnostics only; never current-frame exposure control

requiredContract: scene-linear shaper when the input is unbounded HDR
gradingLut.defaultPlacementWhenToneMappedLinear: after tone mapping and before the sole output conversion

defaultGraphOrder: stable scene-linear lighting, AO, atmosphere, and eligible layers
optional temporal reconstruction of stable scene radiance
transparent or refractive layers excluded from temporal history
optional exposure meter tap from resolved pre-bloom scene-linear HDR
optional bloom and other scene-linear optical effects
optional grading in its declared domain

conditionalConsumes: velocity only when cloud temporal reconstruction is enabled and coverage is validated
velocity contract only when a temporal consumer is enabled
only the named MRT outputs and histories selected by the runtime graph
